entities.description: P.T. Barnum (1810-1891) is the author and the voice of "[[The Art of Money Getting]]" (1880). He presents his advice based on personal experience as a successful showman and entrepreneur. Barnum confe

entities.title: Tobacco
entities.description: Barnum treats tobacco as an artificial appetite deliberately cultivated against human nature. He argues that no creature except worms naturally love tobacco, yet people deliberately form the habit. On

entities.title: Intoxication
entities.description: Barnum contends that business requires a clear brain to reason through plans carefully, yet intoxicating drinks muddle the brain and create lassitude, neutralizing the energy essential to success. The

entities.title: Speculation

entities.title: Integrity

entities.title: Perseverance
entities.description: Barnum identifies perseverance as the critical factor separating success from near-victory that collapses at the threshold. Many reach near the goal but lose faith and relax efforts, losing the prize

entities.title: Mrs. Grundy

entities.title: Excellence
entities.description: Barnum asserts that whoever excels all others in their [[Vocation]] and maintains good habits and [[Integrity]] cannot fail to secure abundant patronage and the natural [[Wealth]] that follows. There

entities.title: Charity
entities.description: Barnum connects charity to business success, arguing that the liberal man who gives to charity will command more patronage than the stingy miser, even if charity is pursued only as business policy. Ge

entities.title: Debt
entities.description: Barnum defines debt as enslavement—it drains self-respect and creates a "slavish position" from which escape becomes nearly impossible. Creditors possess "perfect memories" and will eventually collect

entities.title: Economy
entities.description: Barnum's foundational principle: keeping expenditure consistently below income through systematic awareness, not penny-pinching. He specifically criticizes false economy—the "penny wise and pound fool

entities.title: Advertising

entities.title: Wealth
entities.description: Barnum treats wealth as achievable through systematic effort following natural principles rather than [[Luck]]. Poor boys who work hard and learn the value of money reliably become wealthy, while rich

entities.title: Vocation
entities.description: Barnum argues that natural diversity in talents means different people have innate aptitudes requiring matched vocational choices. Forcing someone into an unsuitable vocation creates lifelong struggle

entities.title: System

entities.title: Capital

entities.title: Health

entities.title: Luck
entities.description: Barnum explicitly argues that [[Luck does not exist]]. Finding gold purses in the street day after day is impossible; success and failure follow from proper methods and causes, not chance. Like causes
